Marta Sandini

Chef & founder

Bermondsey, London

Opening 2026

The chef

Formed by
three continents.

Marta Sandini trained under Ana Roš at Hiša Franko — Slovenia's two-Michelin-starred restaurant — then led the kitchen at Naughty Piglets, one of London's most critically respected small rooms. She cooked at Luca and Allegra, and spent years in China building a structural understanding of fermentation that runs through everything she makes.

Her food is Italian in technique, Chinese in depth, and entirely her own. A dashi-pickled tomato. A wonton filled with nduja. Dishes that feel inevitable.

“One of the most focused, most personal kitchens in London.”

The Guardian

The food

Vegetable and fish-forward.
Meat chosen for quality,
not quantity.

Salmon · nduja emulsion
Pasta · sage butter
Braised short rib · aged black vinegar
Prawns · fermented
Fish · floral plate · kombu butter
How she cooks

Fermentation
is the foundation.

Every service begins in the fermentation room — not in the kitchen. Live misos, house-pickled alliums, preserved vegetables, kombu. Each batch is dated and documented. The pantry is built before the menu exists.

This is not a garnish or a flavour note. It is the structural logic of how Marta cooks. Restraint comes from having the depth already in place before a plate is assembled.

CHIETO  —  Bermondsey  —  2026
